title: Workflow Runtime Config
description: Runtime configuration fields injected by the orchestrator's Build Topic Context node at the start of every per-topic daily run. All downstream sub-workflows receive these fields as part of the run context. Downstream modules are expected to prefer ctx.ai_provider and ctx.media_mode as the validated run-level configuration so that a single, validated config governs the entire run, even though some existing workflows may still re-read $vars.AI_PROVIDER or $vars.MEDIA_MODE directly.

ai_provider.description: Active AI provider for the run. Resolved from the AI_PROVIDER n8n variable. Defaults to 'openai' when AI_PROVIDER is not set or is empty. Reserved as the run-level provider selector for workflow branching. Currently used by the media generation branch (06b–06d); AI sub-workflows 02–07 continue to use the OpenAI path unless they are explicitly updated to branch on ai_provider.

media_mode.description: Active media output mode for the run. Resolved from the MEDIA_MODE n8n variable. Defaults to 'image_video' when MEDIA_MODE is not set or is empty. Determines which branch of the media pipeline runs after step 06 (Video Script). See config/media-mode.json for the full mode definitions and provider capability matrix.

defaults.ai_provider: openai — safe default; no AI_PROVIDER variable required for standard operation
defaults.media_mode: image_video — safe default; the only fully available media strategy in v1

branching.image_video: Runs 06b Generate Images → 06c Generate Narration → 06d Render Video. Both openai and google are compatible.
branching.full_video: Not available in v1. Requires a provider with fullVideoGeneration capability. Fails validation until a compatible provider is configured.

validation.description: Both ai_provider and media_mode are validated by the Build Topic Context node before any sub-workflow runs. An incompatible combination (e.g. full_video with openai) throws MEDIA_MODE_CONFIG_ERROR and aborts the run immediately — no partial output is produced.

environment_notes.local: Set AI_PROVIDER and MEDIA_MODE in n8n Settings → Variables. For local development both can be left unset — defaults (openai / image_video) are used automatically.
environment_notes.staging: Set AI_PROVIDER=openai and MEDIA_MODE=image_video unless you are specifically testing the Google provider path. Set RENDER_PROVIDER=none if you do not have render credentials.
environment_notes.production: Set AI_PROVIDER and MEDIA_MODE explicitly. Do not rely on defaults in production. Set RENDER_PROVIDER=shotstack or RENDER_PROVIDER=creatomate with the corresponding API key.
